title: atalantia malabarica
intro: Atalantia malabarica, also known as the Atalantia fruit or Indian plum, is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Atalantia malabarica are numerous, including the potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve digestion, and support immune function, as well as its use as an anti-diabetic and anti-cancer agent. In herbal preparations, Atalantia malabarica is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or extracts, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
